source_repo: upstream lazygit checkout used for parity research

bindings.info: View options for the command log e.g. show/hide the command log and focus the command log.
Push the current branch to its upstream branch. If no upstream is configured, you will be prompted to configure an upstream branch.
Pull changes from the remote for the current branch. If no upstream is configured, you will be prompted to configure an upstream branch.
Increase the similarity threshold for a deletion and addition pair to be treated as a rename. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'git.renameSimilarityThreshold'.
Decrease the similarity threshold for a deletion and addition pair to be treated as a rename. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'git.renameSimilarityThreshold'.
Increase the amount of the context shown around changes in the diff view. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'git.diffContextSize'.
Decrease the amount of the context shown around changes in the diff view. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'git.diffContextSize'.
Bring up a prompt where you can enter a shell command to execute.
View options to abort/continue/skip the current merge/rebase.
Refresh the git state (i.e. run `git status`, `git branch`, etc in background to update the contents of panels). This does not run `git fetch`.
Choose the next pager in the list of configured pagers
View options for filtering the commit log, so that only commits matching the filter are shown.
View options relating to diffing two refs e.g. diffing against selected ref, entering ref to diff against, and reversing the diff direction.
View options relating to diffing two refs e.g. diffing against selected ref, entering ref to diff against, and reversing the diff direction.
Toggle whether or not whitespace changes are shown in the diff view. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'git.ignoreWhitespaceInDiffView'.
The reflog will be used to determine what git command to run to undo the last git command. This does not include changes to the working tree; only commits are taken into consideration.
The reflog will be used to determine what git command to run to redo the last git command. This does not include changes to the working tree; only commits are taken into consideration.

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Checkout file. This replaces the file in your working tree with the version from the selected commit.
Discard this commit's changes to this file. This runs an interactive rebase in the background, so you may get a merge conflict if a later commit also changes this file.
Open file in default application.
Open file in external editor.
Toggle whether the file is included in the custom patch. See https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit#rebase-magic-custom-patches.
Add/remove all commit's files to custom patch. See https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit#rebase-magic-custom-patches.
If a file is selected, enter the file so that you can add/remove individual lines to the custom patch. If a directory is selected, toggle the directory.

Toggle file view between flat and tree layout. Flat layout shows all file paths in a single list, tree layout groups files by directory. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'gui.showFileTree'.
Collapse all directories in the files tree
Expand all directories in the file tree

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text
Copy abbreviated commit hash to clipboard

bindings.info: Squash the selected commit into the commit below it. The selected commit's message will be appended to the commit below it.
Meld the selected commit into the commit below it. Similar to squash, but the selected commit's message will be discarded.
Set the message option for the fixup commit. The -C option means to use this commit's message instead of the target commit's message.
Reword the selected commit's message.
Drop the selected commit. This will remove the commit from the branch via a rebase. If the commit makes changes that later commits depend on, you may need to resolve merge conflicts.

Edit the selected commit. Use this to start an interactive rebase from the selected commit. When already mid-rebase, this will mark the selected commit for editing, which means that upon continuing the rebase, the rebase will pause at the selected commit to allow you to make changes.

Start an interactive rebase for the commits on your branch. This will include all commits from the HEAD commit down to the first merge commit or main branch commit.
If you would instead like to start an interactive rebase from the selected commit, press `e`.
Mark the selected commit to be picked (when mid-rebase). This means that the commit will be retained upon continuing the rebase.
Create 'fixup!' commit for the selected commit. Later on, you can press `S` on this same commit to apply all above fixup commits.
Squash all 'fixup!' commits, either above the selected commit, or all in current branch (autosquash).

bindings.action: Mark as base commit for rebase
bindings.info: Select a base commit for the next rebase. When you rebase onto a branch, only commits above the base commit will be brought across. This uses the `git rebase --onto` command.
Amend commit with staged changes. If the selected commit is the HEAD commit, this will perform `git commit --amend`. Otherwise the commit will be amended via a rebase.
Set/Reset commit author or set co-author.
Create a revert commit for the selected commit, which applies the selected commit's changes in reverse.
Create a new tag pointing at the selected commit. You'll be prompted to enter a tag name and optional description.
View options for commit log e.g. changing sort order, hiding the git graph, showing the whole git graph.
Checkout the selected commit as a detached HEAD.
Copy commit attribute to clipboard (e.g. hash, URL, diff, message, author).

bindings.action: Create new branch off of commit

bindings.info: Create a new branch and move the unpushed commits of the current branch to it. Useful if you meant to start new work and forgot to create a new branch first. Note that this disregards the selection, the new branch is always created either from the main branch or stacked on top of the current branch (you get to choose which).
View reset options (soft/mixed/hard) for resetting onto selected item.

Mark commit as copied. Then, within the local commits view, you can press `V` to paste (cherry-pick) the copied commit(s) into your checked out branch. At any time you can press `<esc>` to cancel the selection.

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Toggle staged for selected file.
Commit staged changes.

Find the commit that your current changes are building upon, for the sake of amending/fixing up the commit. This spares you from having to look through your branch's commits one-by-one to see which commit should be amended/fixed up. See docs: <https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit/tree/master/docs/Fixup_Commits.md>
Open file in external editor.
Open file in default application.
Stash all changes. For other variations of stashing, use the view stash options keybinding.
View stash options (e.g. stash all, stash staged, stash unstaged).
Toggle staged/unstaged for all files in working tree.
If the selected item is a file, focus the staging view so you can stage individual hunks/lines. If the selected item is a directory, collapse/expand it.
View options for discarding changes to the selected file.
View reset options for working tree (e.g. nuking the working tree).

Toggle file view between flat and tree layout. Flat layout shows all file paths in a single list, tree layout groups files by directory. The default can be changed in the config file with the key 'gui.showFileTree'.
View options for resolving merge conflicts.
Fetch changes from remote.
Collapse all directories in the files tree
Expand all directories in the file tree

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Checkout selected item.

Create a new branch and move the unpushed commits of the current branch to it. Useful if you meant to start new work and forgot to create a new branch first. Note that this disregards the selection, the new branch is always created either from the main branch or stacked on top of the current branch (you get to choose which).

bindings.action: Copy pull request URL to clipboard

bindings.info: Checkout by name. In the input box you can enter '-' to switch to the previous branch.
Force checkout selected branch. This will discard all local changes in your working directory before checking out the selected branch.
View delete options for local/remote branch.
Rebase the checked-out branch onto the selected branch.
View options for merging the selected item into the current branch (regular merge, squash merge)
Fast-forward selected branch from its upstream.
View options relating to the branch's upstream e.g. setting/unsetting the upstream and resetting to the upstream.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Undo last merge conflict resolution.
Open file in external editor.
Open file in default application.
View options for resolving merge conflicts.
Switch to other view (staged/unstaged changes).

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Toggle line-by-line vs. hunk selection mode.
Open file in default application.
Open file in external editor.
Remove the selected lines from this commit. This runs an interactive rebase in the background, so you may get a merge conflict if a later commit also changes these lines.

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Toggle line-by-line vs. hunk selection mode.
Toggle selection staged / unstaged.
When unstaged change is selected, discard the change using `git reset`. When staged change is selected, unstage the change.
Open file in default application.
Open file in external editor.
Switch to other view (staged/unstaged changes).
Edit selected hunk in external editor.
Commit staged changes.

Find the commit that your current changes are building upon, for the sake of amending/fixing up the commit. This spares you from having to look through your branch's commits one-by-one to see which commit should be amended/fixed up. See docs: <https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit/tree/master/docs/Fixup_Commits.md>

bindings.action: Search the current view by text
Filter the current view by text
Copy abbreviated commit hash to clipboard

bindings.info: Checkout the selected commit as a detached HEAD.
Copy commit attribute to clipboard (e.g. hash, URL, diff, message, author).

bindings.action: Create new branch off of commit

bindings.info: Create a new branch and move the unpushed commits of the current branch to it. Useful if you meant to start new work and forgot to create a new branch first. Note that this disregards the selection, the new branch is always created either from the main branch or stacked on top of the current branch (you get to choose which).
View reset options (soft/mixed/hard) for resetting onto selected item.

Mark commit as copied. Then, within the local commits view, you can press `V` to paste (cherry-pick) the copied commit(s) into your checked out branch. At any time you can press `<esc>` to cancel the selection.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Checkout a new local branch based on the selected remote branch, or the remote branch as a detached head.
View options for merging the selected item into the current branch (regular merge, squash merge)
Rebase the checked-out branch onto the selected branch.
Delete the remote branch from the remote.
Set the selected remote branch as the upstream of the checked-out branch.
View reset options (soft/mixed/hard) for resetting onto selected item.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Remove the selected remote. Any local branches tracking a remote branch from the remote will be unaffected.
Edit the selected remote's name or URL.
Fetch updates from the remote repository. This retrieves new commits and branches without merging them into your local branches.
Quickly add a fork remote by replacing the owner in the origin URL and optionally check out a branch from new remote.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Switch to other view (staged/unstaged changes).

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Apply the stash entry to your working directory.
Apply the stash entry to your working directory and remove the stash entry.
Remove the stash entry from the stash list.

Create a new branch from the selected stash entry. This works by git checking out the commit that the stash entry was created from, creating a new branch from that commit, then applying the stash entry to the new branch as an additional commit.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Open file in default application.
Open file in external editor.

bindings.action: Copy abbreviated commit hash to clipboard

bindings.info: Checkout the selected commit as a detached HEAD.
Copy commit attribute to clipboard (e.g. hash, URL, diff, message, author).

bindings.action: Create new branch off of commit

bindings.info: Create a new branch and move the unpushed commits of the current branch to it. Useful if you meant to start new work and forgot to create a new branch first. Note that this disregards the selection, the new branch is always created either from the main branch or stacked on top of the current branch (you get to choose which).
View reset options (soft/mixed/hard) for resetting onto selected item.

Mark commit as copied. Then, within the local commits view, you can press `V` to paste (cherry-pick) the copied commit(s) into your checked out branch. At any time you can press `<esc>` to cancel the selection.

bindings.action: Search the current view by text

bindings.info: Enter submodule. After entering the submodule, you can press `<esc>` to escape back to the parent repo.
Remove the selected submodule and its corresponding directory.
Update selected submodule.
Initialize the selected submodule to prepare for fetching. You probably want to follow this up by invoking the 'update' action to fetch the submodule.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Checkout the selected tag as a detached HEAD.
Create new tag from current commit. You'll be prompted to enter a tag name and optional description.
View delete options for local/remote tag.
Push the selected tag to a remote. You'll be prompted to select a remote.
View reset options (soft/mixed/hard) for resetting onto selected item.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

bindings.info: Switch to the selected worktree.
Remove the selected worktree. This will both delete the worktree's directory, as well as metadata about the worktree in the .git directory.

bindings.action: Filter the current view by text

config_default_keybindings_go:
Keybinding: KeybindingConfig{
			Universal: KeybindingUniversalConfig{
				Quit:                              "q",
				QuitAlt1:                          "<ctrl+c>",
				SuspendApp:                        "<ctrl+z>",
				Return:                            "<esc>",
				QuitWithoutChangingDirectory:      "Q",
				TogglePanel:                       "<tab>",
				PrevItem:                          "<up>",
				NextItem:                          "<down>",
				PrevItemAlt:                       "k",
				NextItemAlt:                       "j",
				PrevPage:                          ",",
				NextPage:                          ".",
				ScrollLeft:                        "H",
				ScrollRight:                       "L",
				GotoTop:                           "<",
				GotoBottom:                        ">",
				GotoTopAlt:                        "<home>",
				GotoBottomAlt:                     "<end>",
				ToggleRangeSelect:                 "v",
				RangeSelectDown:                   "<shift+down>",
				RangeSelectUp:                     "<shift+up>",
				PrevBlock:                         "<left>",
				NextBlock:                         "<right>",
				PrevBlockAlt:                      "h",
				NextBlockAlt:                      "l",
				PrevBlockAlt2:                     "<backtab>",
				NextBlockAlt2:                     "<tab>",
				JumpToBlock:                       []string{"1", "2", "3", "4", "5"},
				FocusMainView:                     "0",
				NextMatch:                         "n",
				PrevMatch:                         "N",
				StartSearch:                       "/",
				MoveWordLeft:                      platformKeyBinding(platform, map[string]string{"darwin": "<alt+left>"}, "<ctrl+left>"),
				MoveWordRight:                     platformKeyBinding(platform, map[string]string{"darwin": "<alt+right>"}, "<ctrl+right>"),
				BackspaceWord:                     platformKeyBinding(platform, map[string]string{"darwin": "<alt+backspace>"}, "<ctrl+backspace>"),
				ForwardDeleteWord:                 platformKeyBinding(platform, map[string]string{"darwin": "<alt+delete>"}, "<ctrl+delete>"),
				OptionMenu:                        "<disabled>",
				OptionMenuAlt1:                    "?",
				Select:                            "<space>",
				GoInto:                            "<enter>",
				Confirm:                           "<enter>",
				ConfirmMenu:                       "<enter>",
				ConfirmSuggestion:                 "<enter>",
				ConfirmInEditor:                   platformKeyBinding(platform, map[string]string{"darwin": "<meta+enter>"}, "<ctrl+enter>"),
				ConfirmInEditorAlt:                "<ctrl+s>",
				Remove:                            "d",
				New:                               "n",
				Edit:                              "e",
				OpenFile:                          "o",
				OpenRecentRepos:                   "<ctrl+r>",
				ScrollUpMain:                      "<pgup>",
				ScrollDownMain:                    "<pgdown>",
				ScrollUpMainAlt1:                  "K",
				ScrollDownMainAlt1:                "J",
				ScrollUpMainAlt2:                  "<ctrl+u>",
				ScrollDownMainAlt2:                "<ctrl+d>",
				ExecuteShellCommand:               ":",
				CreateRebaseOptionsMenu:           "m",
				Push:                              "P",
				Pull:                              "p",
				Refresh:                           "R",
				CreatePatchOptionsMenu:            "<ctrl+p>",
				NextTab:                           "]",
				PrevTab:                           "[",
				NextScreenMode:                    "+",
				PrevScreenMode:                    "_",
				CyclePagers:                       "|",
				Undo:                              "z",
				Redo:                              "Z",
				FilteringMenu:                     "<ctrl+s>",
				DiffingMenu:                       "W",
				DiffingMenuAlt:                    "<ctrl+e>",
				CopyToClipboard:                   "<ctrl+o>",
				SubmitEditorText:                  "<enter>",
				ExtrasMenu:                        "@",
				ToggleWhitespaceInDiffView:        "<ctrl+w>",
				IncreaseContextInDiffView:         "}",
				DecreaseContextInDiffView:         "{",
				IncreaseRenameSimilarityThreshold: ")",
				DecreaseRenameSimilarityThreshold: "(",
				OpenDiffTool:                      "<ctrl+t>",
			},
			Status: KeybindingStatusConfig{
				CheckForUpdate:             "u",
				RecentRepos:                "<enter>",
				AllBranchesLogGraph:        "a",
				AllBranchesLogGraphReverse: "A",
			},
			Files: KeybindingFilesConfig{
				CommitChanges:            "c",
				CommitChangesWithoutHook: "w",
				AmendLastCommit:          "A",
				CommitChangesWithEditor:  "C",
				FindBaseCommitForFixup:   "<ctrl+f>",
				IgnoreFile:               "i",
				RefreshFiles:             "r",
				StashAllChanges:          "s",
				ViewStashOptions:         "S",
				ToggleStagedAll:          "a",
				ViewResetOptions:         "D",
				Fetch:                    "f",
				ToggleTreeView:           "`",
				OpenMergeOptions:         "M",
				OpenStatusFilter:         "<ctrl+b>",
				ConfirmDiscard:           "x",
				CopyFileInfoToClipboard:  "y",
				CollapseAll:              "-",
				ExpandAll:                "=",
			},
			Branches: KeybindingBranchesConfig{
				CopyPullRequestURL:       "<ctrl+y>",
				CreatePullRequest:        "o",
				ViewPullRequestOptions:   "O",
				OpenPullRequestInBrowser: "G",
				CheckoutBranchByName:     "c",
				ForceCheckoutBranch:      "F",
				CheckoutPreviousBranch:   "-",
				RebaseBranch:             "r",
				RenameBranch:             "R",
				MergeIntoCurrentBranch:   "M",
				MoveCommitsToNewBranch:   "N",
				ViewGitFlowOptions:       "i",
				FastForward:              "f",
				CreateTag:                "T",
				PushTag:                  "P",
				SetUpstream:              "u",
				FetchRemote:              "f",
				AddForkRemote:            "F",
				SortOrder:                "s",
			},
			Worktrees: KeybindingWorktreesConfig{
				ViewWorktreeOptions: "w",
			},
			Commits: KeybindingCommitsConfig{
				SquashDown:                     "s",
				RenameCommit:                   "r",
				RenameCommitWithEditor:         "R",
				ViewResetOptions:               "g",
				MarkCommitAsFixup:              "f",
				SetFixupMessage:                "c",
				CreateFixupCommit:              "F",
				SquashAboveCommits:             "S",
				MoveDownCommit:                 "<ctrl+j>",
				MoveUpCommit:                   "<ctrl+k>",
				AmendToCommit:                  "A",
				ResetCommitAuthor:              "a",
				PickCommit:                     "p",
				RevertCommit:                   "t",
				CherryPickCopy:                 "C",
				PasteCommits:                   "V",
				MarkCommitAsBaseForRebase:      "B",
				CreateTag:                      "T",
				CheckoutCommit:                 "<space>",
				ResetCherryPick:                "<ctrl+r>",
				CopyCommitAttributeToClipboard: "y",
				OpenLogMenu:                    "<ctrl+l>",
				OpenInBrowser:                  "o",
				OpenPullRequestInBrowser:       "G",
				ViewBisectOptions:              "b",
				StartInteractiveRebase:         "i",
				SelectCommitsOfCurrentBranch:   "*",
			},
			AmendAttribute: KeybindingAmendAttributeConfig{
				ResetAuthor: "a",
				SetAuthor:   "A",
				AddCoAuthor: "c",
			},
			Stash: KeybindingStashConfig{
				PopStash:    "g",
				RenameStash: "r",
			},
			CommitFiles: KeybindingCommitFilesConfig{
				CheckoutCommitFile: "c",
			},
			Main: KeybindingMainConfig{
				ToggleSelectHunk: "a",
				PickBothHunks:    "b",
				EditSelectHunk:   "E",
			},
			Submodules: KeybindingSubmodulesConfig{
				Init:     "i",
				Update:   "u",
				BulkMenu: "b",
			},
			CommitMessage: KeybindingCommitMessageConfig{
				CommitMenu: "<ctrl+o>",
			},
		},
